channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-16
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. Notably, the **scienceverse/metacheck** and **giladfeldman/docpluck** projects have made substantial updates, with additions such as zenodo citations, extraction guardrails, and fallback telemetry counters. Meanwhile, **Davidvandijcke/coarse** has integrated CodeQL analysis into its workflow, and **JamieCummins/regcheck** has improved report viewing and added quality of life features. Additionally, **giladfeldman/escicheck** has synced with various effectcheck versions, and **shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve** has revised its validation note and included a citation. These updates demonstrate the connections and collaborations within our community, and we celebrate the progress being made across these projects.

88 commits across 5 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Most active:

metacheck31 commits
docpluck26 commits
regcheck19 commits
coarse11 commits
auto-zcurve1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 4 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

Work was done on the 'main' and 'dev' branches. On the 'dev' branch, a "zenodo citation" was added, as stated in the commit message. The 'dev' branch also received an update to "apps and docs", according to the commit message. Additionally, pull requests #310, #311, and #312 were merged into the 'dev' and 'main' branches, with #310 originating from 'nielsvanberkel/main'. The 'dev' branch was also merged into the 'main' branch. The 'main' branch received merges from the 'dev' branch via pull requests #311 and #312.

4 commit(s) today:

  • a7dea29 [main] Merge pull request #312 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • f09fb47 [dev] zenodo citation — Lisa DeBruine
  • 00eaa3b [main] Merge pull request #311 from scienceverse/dev — Lisa DeBruine
  • 767da17 [dev] update apps and docs — Lisa DeBruine
  • 9d012e7 [dev] Merge pull request #310 from nielsvanberkel/main — Lisa DeBruine
  • 8a605a3 [dev] Merge branch 'dev' into main — Lisa DeBruine
  • 3e127fa [dev] Replace devtools::install_github with pak::pkg_install — nielsvanberkel
  • 9ef4d94 [prereg_regcheck] name fix — Lakens
  • 2b0b4e4 [prereg_regcheck] working version of Regcheck — Lakens
  • 9a007d9 [dev] TUE server uses 0.9.0 GROBID now as well — Lakens
  • 920c2d9 [dev] Add vignette describing all references — Lakens
  • 97bb1c1 [dev] stat_effect_size: detect tests via extract_eq(); add implied sample sizes (#308) — Lakens
  • f495921 [dev] clean up website — Lisa DeBruine
  • a6c4cd3 [dev] mocking updates — Lisa DeBruine
  • d85d73f [dev] Add TUE grobid server — Lisa DeBruine
  • 2b22472 [prereg_regcheck] Align reg_check module style with the other modules — Lakens
  • d6e221d [prereg_regcheck] Fix regcheck multipart dimensions format (JSON array, not CSV) — Lakens
  • 7be3aa2 [prereg_regcheck] Add reg_check module: RegCheck preregistration-paper comparison — Lakens
  • fe37300 [dev] add email — Lakens
  • f0f26ef [dev] Shiny app updates and related fixes — Lisa DeBruine

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 17 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, where a feature was added to include "optional extraction guardrails for size and timeout". On the same branch, the documentation was updated to "queue investigation for items 3 and 8" and to "sync normalization and benchmark claims with current code", as well as adding a "root README landing page". The `main` branch also saw a refactor to "surface fallback exception classes in method metadata" and the removal of an "orphaned duplicate band re-extractor" in the `extract_columns` section. Additionally, the `main` branch received a feature to "add fallback telemetry counters and optional logging", and a continuous integration change to "add docs-code consistency guard". The documentation on the `main` branch was further updated in an iterative process, including a "2026-06-16 resume handoff + concurrency-collision lesson".

17 commit(s) today:

  • cfe86f7 [main] feat: add optional extraction guardrails for size and timeout — Gilad Feldman
  • aa82a25 [main] docs(todo): queue investigation for items 3 and 8 — Gilad Feldman
  • 617db41 [main] refactor: surface fallback exception classes in method metadata — Gilad Feldman
  • 24aa793 [main] feat: add fallback telemetry counters and optional logging — Gilad Feldman
  • a5da768 [main] ci: add docs-code consistency guard — Gilad Feldman
  • f8594c3 [main] docs: sync normalization and benchmark claims with current code — Gilad Feldman
  • c8800bd [main] docs: add root README landing page — Gilad Feldman
  • 4c55d6e [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-16 resume handoff + concurrency-collision lesson — Gilad Feldman
  • 436fc5a [main] refactor(extract_columns): remove orphaned duplicate band re-extractor — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The `staging` branch was the focus of recent work, where the model requirements were updated, as indicated by the commit message "update model reqs". Additionally on the `staging` branch, report viewing was improved, along with the addition of quality of life (qol) features, as stated in the commit "improve report viewing + qol features". No other branches were worked on according to the provided commit history. The changes made to the model requirements and report viewing are specific to the `staging` branch, with no other branches mentioned. The quality of life features added are also part of the work done on the `staging` branch. Overall, the work was confined to a single branch, `staging`, with two distinct areas of change.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 9074b4b [staging] update model reqs — Jamie Cummins
  • 76b8a02 [staging] improve report viewing + qol features — Jamie Cummins
  • a90ee3f [staging] improve report UI flow + UX — Jamie Cummins
  • 9240258 [staging] improve aesthetics, report navigation — Jamie Cummins
  • 395a038 [staging] improve report naming — Jamie Cummins
  • bf01cf6 [staging] improve security and sharing of reports — Jamie Cummins
  • 608907d [staging] add profile memory + report editing — Jamie Cummins
  • 670547e [staging] first pass at oauth implementation — Jamie Cummins
  • bcd1fe9 [staging] reformat footer — Jamie Cummins
  • a14ee5b [staging] add dimension fixes — Jamie Cummins
  • 9d12737 [staging] add more robust quote location — Jamie Cummins
  • 3ace417 [staging] merge report formats — Jamie Cummins
  • 00cc087 [staging] Revert "checkpoint: redesign of report page" — Jamie Cummins
  • c3bf9da [staging] update of report — Jamie Cummins
  • dc0a1e6 [staging] checkpoint: redesign of report page — Jamie Cummins
  • c8cc364 [staging] remove footer + modify aesthetics — Jamie Cummins
  • 2b3fe67 [staging] add panelling variation — Jamie Cummins
  • 3a0f1fc [staging] update colour palette — Jamie Cummins
  • b5bda77 [staging] add redesign — Jamie Cummins
  • bdb961d [main] update api (straight to prod :-) ) — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →
